work_unit: ES-H002: studio-precheck.sh shell injection via bash-interpolated python3 -c
situation: audit-2026-07-04-001 finding ES-H002 (priority 2, route-fix): studio-precheck.sh L19-25 and L43-49 embed $CAL_FILE/$STATUS_FILE bash variables directly into python3 -c string literals. Paths containing single quotes or adversarial content break Python syntax and enable arbitrary code execution.
action: Replace both python3 -c invocations with env-var passing pattern: CAL_FILE="$CAL_FILE" python3 -c '... os.environ["CAL_FILE"] ...'. Eliminates interpolation entirely while preserving identical runtime semantics. Verified: bash -n syntax OK, functional tests pass for normal paths AND single-quote-containing paths (original vuln trigger).
outcome_evidence: Worktree commit 2dfc99b on auto/opt-precheck-1783098333 pushed to origin. bash -n clean. Functional test: cooldown=5 path returns skip:cooldown; quoted-path /tmp/t'q with cooldown=4 returns skip:cooldown (previously would have crashed or injected). Main copy reverted by hook as expected (main never modified directly).
next_suggestion: 派生 fix #2: ES-H003 (priority 3) — decision-observer.py confirm_studio_draft non-atomic safe_write_json. Same vuln pattern as stop-completion-gate case-404.
